description: Implement Stripe subscription billing for SEIDO with per-lot pricing (5EUR HT/lot/month or 50EUR HT/lot/year), 30-day app-managed trial, annual-aggressive pricing push, zero-friction upgrades, read-only mode on expiry. TDD approach: test files written BEFORE implementation code.

dashboard.product: Seido subscription test (2 tarifs: 50EUR/an + 5EUR/mois per unit)
dashboard.customerPortal: Configure (carte, annulation, factures — switch plan desactive)
dashboard.coupon: SEIDOBETA26 (25% permanent, expire 1 avr 2026, code promo actif)

testStrategy: TDD — Each epic has a dedicated test story (T-xxx) BEFORE its implementation stories. Tests are written first with expected behavior, then implementation makes them pass.
REGLE_CRITIQUE_TESTS: OBLIGATOIRE: Avant d'ecrire le moindre test Stripe, TOUJOURS consulter la documentation officielle Stripe Testing. Ne JAMAIS deviner le comportement de l'API Stripe — se referer aux docs officielles pour les patterns de mock, les test cards, les signatures webhook, et les fixtures.

mandatory_reading_before_any_test: https://docs.stripe.com/testing — Page principale testing (test mode, test clocks, fixtures)
https://docs.stripe.com/test-mode — Fonctionnement du mode test Stripe (cles sk_test/pk_test)
https://docs.stripe.com/testing#cards — Cartes de test (4242... success, 4000000000000341 decline, etc.)
https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks#test-webhook — Tester les webhooks avec Stripe CLI (stripe listen --forward-to)
https://docs.stripe.com/stripe-cli — Installation et usage de Stripe CLI pour tests locaux
https://docs.stripe.com/billing/testing/test-clocks — Test Clocks pour simuler le temps (trial expiry, renewals)

test_cards_reference.requires_auth_3ds: 4000002500003155 (3D Secure required — triggers incomplete status if abandoned)

webhook_testing.signature_verification: stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig, webhookSecret) — TOUJOURS verifier en test
webhook_testing.mock_pattern: vi.mock('stripe') puis mock constructEvent pour retourner l'event desire sans signature reelle

test_clocks_for_trials.description: Stripe Test Clocks permettent d'avancer le temps pour tester trial expiry sans attendre 30 jours
test_clocks_for_trials.note: Utile pour T-009 (CRON tests) mais pas obligatoire si on mock la date

corrections: C1: Table subscriptions = CREATE TABLE (pas ALTER — table n'existe pas encore)
C2: Admin client = getSupabaseAdmin() dans lib/services/core/supabase-admin.ts
C3: Email templates dans emails/templates/ (pas lib/email/templates/)
C4: Billing access = role admin dans team_members uniquement
C5: Coupons = pourcentage uniquement, duree flexible, Stripe Dashboard. Coupon actuel: SEIDOBETA26 (25% permanent, expire 1 avr 2026). Autres coupons a creer plus tard si besoin.
C6: Read-only impacte locataires/prestataires avec message clair
C7: Double page billing : resume parametres + page dediee /gestionnaire/abonnement
C8: Notification suppression lot + lien Portal (pas de reduction auto)
C9: TVA = HT (prix affiches hors taxes)
C10: Trial demarre a la creation team (signup gestionnaire)
C11: Beta users : trial 30j a partir du deploiement Stripe
C12: Enum subscription_status doit inclure 'paused' (pause subscription via Portal — design doc 14.12)
C13: Webhook handler doit couvrir charge.refunded et customer.subscription.paused (design doc 3.4)
C14: State machine = 12 transitions (pas 8) — inclure incomplete, incomplete_expired, unpaid, free_tier→active
C15: Email templates: ajouter J+7 feature, J+14 value, J+3 win-back (design doc 8.1, 6 templates manquants)
C16: E2E checkout doit tester carte 3D Secure (4000002500003155) + decline (4000000000000002)
C17: Webhook out-of-order: handler doit fetch+reconcile si DB state ne matche pas Stripe state (design doc 9.5)

testInfrastructure.mockPattern: vi.mock() for Stripe SDK + Supabase client. Service tests inject mock repositories.
testInfrastructure.existingTestFiles: 51 unit tests (3 files), 35 integration tests (4 files), 20+ E2E tests

testConventions.unit: tests/unit/stripe/*.test.ts — pure function tests, no real API calls
testConventions.e2e: tests/e2e/stripe/*.e2e.ts — full browser flows with Stripe test mode

userStories.title: Installation packages Stripe
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Stripe SDK installed and configured so that I can interact with Stripe API from the SEIDO codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: Exports: stripe instance, STRIPE_PRICES, FREE_TIER_LIMIT, TRIAL_DAYS, calculatePrice(), calculateAnnualSavings()
userStories.notes: lib/stripe.ts created with lazy getStripe() pattern, STRIPE_PRICES, calculatePrice, calculateAnnualSavings. Lint passes.

userStories.title: TESTS: lib/stripe.ts — calculatePrice, calculateAnnualSavings, constants
userStories.description: TDD: Write unit tests for the Stripe utility library BEFORE implementation. Tests define expected behavior for pricing calculations and constants.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests calculatePrice(): free tier (0-2 lots = 0), monthly (3+ lots), annual (3+ lots), edge cases (0 lots, negative)
Tests calculateAnnualSavings(): correct savings formula, edge cases
Tests fail initially (RED phase) — no implementation yet
userStories.notes: 18/18 tests passing. Tests: constants (FREE_TIER_LIMIT=2, TRIAL_DAYS=30, STRIPE_PRICES shape), calculatePrice (free tier 0-2, monthly 3+, annual 3+, 100 lots, negative), calculateAnnualSavings (0 lots, 3 lots, 10 lots, 17% discount, negative).

userStories.title: Migration DB — Schema Stripe complet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Stripe-related database tables and functions created so that the subscription system has its data layer ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Enum subscription_status cree (trialing, active, past_due, canceled, incomplete, incomplete_expired, free_tier, read_only, unpaid, paused)
RLS policies: SELECT pour role admin sur subscriptions, stripe_customers, stripe_invoices
userStories.notes: Migration 20260221000000 applied. 4 tables, 10-value enum, trigger, 3 helper functions, RLS (admin only, tm.left_at IS NULL), 8 indexes. Types regenerated.

userStories.title: Migration donnees beta users
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want existing beta users to receive a 30-day trial from Stripe deployment date so that they have time to evaluate the paid offering.
acceptanceCriteria: Script migration cree record subscriptions pour chaque team existante
Stripe Customer cree pour chaque team
userStories.notes: Migration 20260221000001 applied. 2 teams migrated (1 free_tier <=2 lots, 1 trialing with 30-day trial). Idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

userStories.title: TESTS: SubscriptionRepository — all CRUD methods
userStories.description: TDD: Write unit tests for SubscriptionRepository BEFORE implementation. Mock Supabase client to test method signatures, query patterns, error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests findByTeamId: returns subscription or null, uses .limit(1).maybeSingle()
Tests create: passes correct data to supabase.from('subscriptions').insert()
Tests update: passes id + data to supabase.from('subscriptions').update().eq()
Tests updateByTeamId: uses team_id not id
Tests getLotCount: returns number from subscriptions.billable_properties
Tests error handling: returns { success: false, error } on Supabase error
Mock pattern: vi.mock('@/lib/services') for Supabase client
userStories.notes: Fused with US-004. 20/20 tests passing. createChainMock() helper pattern for Supabase query chain mocking.

userStories.title: TESTS: StripeCustomerRepository — all methods
userStories.description: TDD: Write unit tests for StripeCustomerRepository BEFORE implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests findByTeamId: returns customer or null
Tests error cases: duplicate stripe_customer_id, missing team_id
userStories.notes: Fused with US-005. 9/9 tests passing (findByTeamId 4, findByStripeCustomerId 2, create 3).

userStories.title: Repository Subscription
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a SubscriptionRepository following the SEIDO Repository Pattern so that subscription data access is centralized and type-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Methodes: findByTeamId, findByStripeSubscriptionId, create, update, updateByTeamId, upsertByTeamId, getLotCount
userStories.notes: Fused with T-002. 7 methods: findByTeamId, findByStripeSubscriptionId, create, update, updateByTeamId, upsertByTeamId, getLotCount. All use .limit(1).maybeSingle().

userStories.title: Repository Stripe Customer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a StripeCustomerRepository so that Stripe customer mapping is managed via the repository pattern.
userStories.notes: Fused with T-003. 3 methods: findByTeamId, findByStripeCustomerId, create.

userStories.title: TESTS: SubscriptionService — state machine + business logic (HEAVY)
userStories.description: TDD: Write comprehensive unit tests for SubscriptionService. This is the most critical test file — covers all 8 status transitions, canAddProperty logic, trial logic, checkout session creation, upgrade flow.
acceptanceCriteria: - active -> canceled (user cancels via Portal, cancel_at_period_end then deleted webhook)
- past_due -> unpaid (dunning exhausted, alternative to canceled per Stripe settings)
- free_tier -> active (adding 3rd lot triggers checkout, new subscription created)
- incomplete -> active (3D Secure completed after initial failure)
- incomplete -> incomplete_expired (23h timeout, first invoice not paid)
- getSubscriptionInfo: returns correct SubscriptionInfo shape for each status
- canAddProperty: true when free_tier+lots<=2, true when active, false when read_only, upgrade_needed when at limit
- getOrCreateStripeCustomer: creates if not exists, returns existing if found (idempotent)
- createCheckoutSession: passes correct params (allow_promotion_codes, annual default, quantity)
- previewUpgrade: calls invoices.createPreview, returns is_estimate flag
- initializeTrialSubscription: creates record with trialing status, no Stripe sub
- isReadOnlyMode: true for read_only status, false for others
- canAddProperty with null subscription (new team) = true (free tier)
- free_tier with exactly 2 lots = can add (still free)
- free_tier with 2 lots adding 1 = needs upgrade (3 lots)
- Stripe API error in hasPaymentMethod = false (graceful fallback)
- previewUpgrade API failure = returns is_estimate: true with disclaimer text
- cancel_at_period_end: subscription remains active until period end, then canceled
- concurrent upgrade: SELECT FOR UPDATE prevents double-charging (race condition)
- paused subscription: read-only mode applied, resume restores active
Mock repositories: inject mock SubscriptionRepository + StripeCustomerRepository
userStories.notes: Fused with US-006. 56/56 tests passing. 10 method groups: getSubscriptionInfo(6), canAddProperty(12), hasPaymentMethod(4), getOrCreateStripeCustomer(3), createCheckoutSession(5), upgradeSubscriptionDirect(5), previewUpgrade(4), createPortalSession(2), initializeTrialSubscription(4), isReadOnlyMode(11). Constructor DI with mock factories.

userStories.title: SubscriptionService — Core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete SubscriptionService with all business logic so that subscription state management is centralized.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 methodes: getSubscriptionInfo, canAddProperty, hasPaymentMethod, getOrCreateStripeCustomer, createCheckoutSession, upgradeSubscriptionDirect, previewUpgrade, createPortalSession, initializeTrialSubscription, isReadOnlyMode
T-004 tests passent (GREEN phase — all 45+ tests)
userStories.notes: Fused with T-004. 10 methods: getSubscriptionInfo, canAddProperty, hasPaymentMethod, getOrCreateStripeCustomer, createCheckoutSession, upgradeSubscriptionDirect, previewUpgrade, createPortalSession, initializeTrialSubscription, isReadOnlyMode. Private helpers: checkReadOnly, checkCanAddProperty. Exported types: SubscriptionInfo, UpgradePreview, CanAddPropertyResult.

userStories.title: TESTS: Webhook handler — signature, idempotency, all event types
userStories.description: TDD: Write integration tests for the Stripe webhook API route. Mock Stripe signature verification, test each event handler, verify idempotency logic.
acceptanceCriteria: - First processing of event_id: processes and returns 200
- Duplicate event_id: skips processing and returns 200
- Insert to stripe_webhook_events BEFORE processing logic
- checkout.session.completed: updates subscription status to active, verifies metadata.team_id
- customer.subscription.updated: updates subscription fields (status, period dates, quantity, cancel_at_period_end)
- customer.subscription.deleted: transitions to read_only or free_tier based on lot count
- charge.refunded: logs refund to activity_logs (partial + full refund)
- Unknown event type: logs and returns 200 (no crash)
- subscription.updated received BEFORE subscription.created: handler fetches current Stripe state and reconciles
- checkout.session.completed with subscription already active: idempotent no-op
- Database error during processing: returns 500 (Stripe retries with exponential backoff up to 3 days)
- Handler completes within 20s budget (no email sending in handler)
- Invalid event data shape: returns 400 (no Stripe retry)
userStories.notes: TDD RED phase. Webhook is security-critical — signature verification + idempotency tests are essential. CORRECTION C13: charge.refunded + subscription.paused added. CORRECTION C17: out-of-order reconciliation tests added. DOCS OBLIGATOIRES: https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks#test-webhook — tester webhooks localement, https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks/signatures — verification signature (constructEvent), https://docs.stripe.com/api/events/types — liste complete des event types et leur payload, https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks#best-practices — idempotency et out-of-order handling, https://docs.stripe.com/stripe-cli — stripe listen + stripe trigger pour tests locaux.

userStories.title: Webhook API Route
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Stripe webhook handler at /api/stripe/webhook so that all Stripe events are securely processed and synced to our DB.
userStories.notes: Fused with T-005. 24/24 tests. StripeWebhookHandler class with 9 event handlers + idempotency via stripe_webhook_events (insert-before-process, 23505 detection). API route at /api/stripe/webhook with constructEvent signature verification. Uses createServiceRoleSupabaseClient. charge.refunded simplified (no activity_logs insert — missing enum value). Out-of-order handling via findByStripeSubscriptionId fallback.

userStories.title: TESTS: Server Actions — auth guards, return shapes, all 9 actions
userStories.description: TDD: Write tests for subscription server actions. Mock auth context, verify each action's behavior and return type.
acceptanceCriteria: - All actions reject when not authenticated
- All actions reject when user role is not admin
- checkCanAddProperty: returns { allowed: boolean, reason?: string, upgrade_needed: boolean }
- createCheckoutSessionAction('monthly'): returns { url: string } with different price
- upgradeSubscriptionDirect(1): returns { success: boolean, invoice_amount?: number }
- verifyCheckoutSession: returns { verified: boolean, subscription_status?: string }
userStories.notes: 26/26 tests passing. Auth guards (9 reject + 1 accept), Return shapes (11 tests), Business logic (5 tests). Mock pattern: regular function constructor for SubscriptionService, getStripe() returns mock with checkout.sessions.retrieve.

userStories.title: Server Actions Subscription
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire admin, I want server actions to manage my team's subscription so that I can check status, upgrade, and manage billing from the app.
acceptanceCriteria: 9 actions: getSubscriptionStatus, checkCanAddProperty, checkHasPaymentMethod, isReadOnlyMode, createCheckoutSessionAction, upgradeSubscriptionDirect, getUpgradePreview, verifyCheckoutSession, createPortalSessionAction
userStories.notes: 9 server actions created with getServerActionAuthContextOrNull('gestionnaire'). createService() helper instantiates SubscriptionService with DI. verifyCheckoutSession uses getStripe() directly for session retrieval. All 26 T-006 tests pass.

userStories.title: TESTS: Trial initialization in signup flow
userStories.description: TDD: Write tests for trial initialization logic called during team creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests initializeTrialSubscription creates correct record (status=trialing, trial_end=+30d)
Tests Stripe Customer created without subscription
Tests <= 2 lots results in free_tier status directly
Tests idempotency: calling twice does not create duplicate records
Tests error handling: Stripe customer creation failure
userStories.notes: 9/9 tests passing. Tests: trialing>2lots, free_tier<=2lots, boundary 2 lots, 0 lots, 30-day trial window, no Stripe API calls, billable_properties, DB error propagation, no stripe_subscription_id.

userStories.title: Integration trial dans le signup flow
userStories.description: As a nouveau gestionnaire, I want my 30-day trial to start automatically when I create my team so that I can use all features without payment info.
acceptanceCriteria: Apres creation team dans callback auth, appel initializeTrialSubscription()
userStories.notes: Migration 20260221000002: Added Step 5 to handle_new_user_confirmed() trigger — INSERT INTO subscriptions(free_tier, trial 30d) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Stripe customer created lazily. Welcome email already handled by confirm-actions.ts.

userStories.title: TESTS: useSubscription hook — cache, refresh, status derivation
userStories.description: TDD: Write tests for the client-side useSubscription hook. Test caching, refresh behavior, derived boolean helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests initial load: calls getSubscriptionStatus, returns loading=true then result
Tests cache: second render does NOT re-fetch
Tests refresh(): forces re-fetch, updates state
Tests hasPaymentMethod(): derives from status or separate call
Tests error handling: action failure sets error state
Uses vi.mock('react') for useState/useEffect simulation OR renderHook from @testing-library/react
userStories.notes: 13/13 tests passing. Tested without @testing-library/react by extracting deriveFromStatus() logic. Tests: 6 derived state, 5 server action delegation, 2 cache/refresh logic.

userStories.title: Hook useSubscription
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a client-side hook for subscription state so that any component can check status reactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose: status, loading, refresh(), canAddProperty(), hasPaymentMethod()
userStories.notes: Hook created at hooks/use-subscription.ts. Exports UseSubscriptionReturn with status, loading, error, refresh, canAddProperty, isReadOnly, isFreeTier, hasStripeSubscription, daysLeftTrial, checkCanAddProperty(), checkHasPaymentMethod(). Uses fetchedRef to prevent duplicate initial fetches.

userStories.title: Pricing Card (annual-aggressive)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire potentiel, I want pricing that highlights annual savings so that I'm incentivized to choose annual.
acceptanceCriteria: Annuel: carte highlight, badge Recommande Economisez 17%, prix barre, CTA primaire
Prix affiches HT + mention TVA applicable
Calcul dynamique total = nb_lots x prix
userStories.notes: Created components/billing/pricing-card.tsx. Annual card: highlighted border, Recommandé badge, Économisez 17% badge, strikethrough monthly price, blue calculation box with dynamic total + monthly equivalent + savings. Monthly: compact single-line card with outline button. Prices HT + TVA mention. Uses calculatePrice/calculateAnnualSavings from lib/stripe.ts.

userStories.title: Page dediee Abonnement
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire admin, I want a dedicated billing page showing my subscription details so that I can see my plan, usage, invoices, and manage my subscription.
acceptanceCriteria: Affiche: statut, plan, lots utilises/abonnes, prochaine facture, date renouvellement
Bouton Gerer mon abonnement → Stripe Portal
Bouton Mettre a niveau si upgrade possible
userStories.notes: Page at /gestionnaire/settings/billing (matches Stripe redirect URLs). Server Component fetches initialSubscriptionInfo, passes to BillingPageClient. Client handles: status card (4 metrics grid), checkout verification, Stripe Portal redirect, PricingCard for non-subscribers. ValueCalculator deferred to US-014.

userStories.title: Resume abonnement dans Parametres
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire admin, I want a subscription summary card in settings so that I can quickly see my plan status.
acceptanceCriteria: Affiche: statut, plan, lots, jours restants trial
Si trial: compte a rebours + CTA annuel
Si read-only: banner rouge + CTA reactivation
userStories.notes: Created components/billing/subscription-summary-card.tsx. Replaced SubscriptionManagementSection in settings-page.tsx. Card shows status badge, 3-col metrics (lots, plan, trial countdown), conditional CTAs (blue for trial, red for read-only), link to billing page.

userStories.title: Trial Banner
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire en trial, I want to see a banner showing my trial progress so that I know when it ends and can subscribe.
acceptanceCriteria: Affiche les 7 derniers jours du trial
userStories.notes: Created trial-banner.tsx. Shows last 7 days only. Color scheme: blue>7d, orange 3-7d, red<=1d. Progress bar + loss-aversion messages. Dismissable 24h via localStorage. CTA to billing page.

userStories.title: Read-Only Banner
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire dont l'essai a expire, I want a permanent banner explaining my read-only status so that I know how to regain access.
acceptanceCriteria: Locataire/prestataire: message adapte (demandes indisponibles, contacter gestionnaire)
userStories.notes: Created read-only-banner.tsx. Role-specific messages: gestionnaire gets upgrade CTAs (annual primary + monthly secondary), locataire sees 'demandes indisponibles', prestataire sees 'nouvelles assignations suspendues'. Permanent, non-dismissable.

userStories.title: Upgrade Modal (double mode)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire atteignant sa limite de lots, I want an upgrade modal that adapts to whether I have a saved card for minimal friction.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode A (CB sauvee): confirmation inline, 1 clic upgradeSubscriptionDirect()
Mode B (pas CB): PricingCard annuel pre-selectionne, Checkout redirect
userStories.notes: Created upgrade-modal.tsx. Mode detection via checkHasPaymentMethod(). Mode A (card saved): shows proration preview + 1-click inline upgrade. Mode B (no card): shows PricingCard with checkout redirect. Disclaimer for estimated amounts. Success/error states.

userStories.title: Contextual Upgrade Prompt
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire bloque par une limite, I want a contextual message explaining why and how to upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: Icone, message, CTA specifique par contexte
userStories.notes: Created upgrade-prompt.tsx. 4 contexts with specific icon/title/description/CTA: add_lot (Building2), add_intervention (Wrench), export_data (Download, no CTA — GDPR), ai_feature (Sparkles). All CTAs route to billing page.

userStories.title: Value Calculator
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire en trial, I want to see how much time and money SEIDO saved me so that I feel the value.
acceptanceCriteria: Affiche: heures economisees, equivalent monetaire, nb interventions
Utilisable dans page abonnement et emails
userStories.notes: Created value-calculator.tsx. 2 variants: 'card' (standalone Card) and 'inline' (horizontal metrics). 3 metrics: interventions count (Wrench/blue), hours saved (Clock/green), money equivalent (Euro/purple). Formula: completedInterventions × 30min × 45€/h.

userStories.title: Integration limite lots dans formulaires
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want lot creation forms to check my subscription limit so that I'm prompted to upgrade if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Formulaire creation lot: check canAddProperty() avant submit
userStories.notes: Modified biens-page-client.tsx: useSubscription hook gates both 'Nouveau lot' and 'Nouvel immeuble' buttons. If canAddProperty=false → opens UpgradeModal. If isReadOnly → buttons disabled + UpgradePrompt shown above property list.

userStories.title: Enforcement read-only cross-app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want read-only mode enforced across the entire app for all roles.
acceptanceCriteria: Locataire: message demandes indisponibles quand team gestionnaire read-only
Prestataire: interventions en cours continuent, pas de nouvelles assignations
userStories.notes: Created subscription-banners.tsx wrapper. Integrated in gestionnaire layout.tsx: fetches subscriptionInfo server-side, renders TrialBanner or ReadOnlyBanner. ReadOnlyBanner has role-specific messages for locataire/prestataire. Biens page buttons disabled in read-only. Export stays accessible (no subscription gate).

userStories.title: Notification suppression lot -> lien Portal
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire qui supprime un lot, I want to be notified I'm still paying for the old quantity.
acceptanceCriteria: Apres suppression lot, si subscribed_lots > actual_lots: toast + bouton Portal
userStories.notes: Modified lot-details-client.tsx: after deletion, checks subscribed_lots > actual_lots → shows sonner toast with 'Gérer' action linking to billing page. Non-blocking try/catch.

userStories.title: Flow Checkout success + verification
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire qui vient de payer, I want payment verified server-side before seeing success.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifie payment_status paid ET team_id correspond
Si non verifie: message verification en cours + polling
Navigation manuelle sans session_id: pas de message succes
userStories.notes: Already implemented in billing-page-client.tsx (US-010): reads ?checkout=success&session_id from URL, calls verifyCheckoutSession(), shows success/verification banner. Navigation without session_id shows nothing.

userStories.title: TESTS: CRON jobs — trial notifications, expiration, behavioral triggers, cleanup
userStories.description: TDD: Write tests for all 4 CRON routes. Test auth, query logic, idempotency, email trigger conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: - J-7: finds trialing subs with trial_end = now+7d, sends email
- J-3: finds trialing subs with trial_end = now+3d, sends email
- J-1: finds trialing subs with trial_end = now+1d, sends email
- Idempotent: skips if notification_j7_sent flag is true
- Does not send to free_tier teams
- Finds expired trials (status=trialing AND trial_end < now)
- Anti-spam: skips if email sent < 7 days ago
- Trial only: skips free_tier and active
- J-7 calculation uses UTC consistently (trial_end stored in UTC)
- Boundary: trial_end at exactly midnight UTC (J-7 = 7*24h, not 6.5 days)
- Win-back email (J+3 post-expiry): sent only to read_only status (not free_tier)
userStories.notes: 45/45 tests passing across 4 files. Tests cover auth, query logic, transition logic, idempotency flags, anti-spam, UTC boundary, email type selection. Total Stripe tests: 218/218.

userStories.title: Email templates billing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all billing email templates created so that CRON jobs and events can send formatted emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Templates dans emails/templates/billing/: welcome (J+1), feature-engagement (J+7), value-report (J+14), trial-ending-7 (J-7), trial-ending-3 (J-3), trial-ending-1 (J-1), trial-expired (J+0), win-back (J+3 post-expiry), payment-failed, subscription-activated
userStories.notes: 8 templates in emails/templates/billing/ + index.ts. 10 billing types added to emails/utils/types.ts. SubscriptionEmailService with 8 send methods + factory. Uses calculatePrice/calculateAnnualSavings for dynamic pricing in trial-ending. All CTAs default to annual. Prix HT + TVA mention.

userStories.title: CRON — Notifications trial (J-7, J-3, J-1)
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want automated emails sent before trial expiry to remind users to subscribe.
acceptanceCriteria: J-1: email critique avec stats + CTA annuel
T-009 trial notifications tests passent (GREEN phase)
userStories.notes: Route created. Iterates J-7/J-3/J-1 windows. Uses .lte() for target date, .is(flag, false) for idempotency. Fetches admin email per team. Sets flag after successful send.

userStories.title: CRON — Expiration trial
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want expired trials automatically transitioned to read-only or free tier.
acceptanceCriteria: T-009 trial expiration tests passent (GREEN phase)
userStories.notes: Route created. Queries status=trialing AND trial_end < now. Transitions to free_tier (<=2 lots) or read_only (>2). Sets trial_expired_email_sent flag. Sends email per team.

userStories.title: CRON — Behavioral triggers
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want usage-based conversion emails for engaged trial users.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggers: >= 3 lots, >= 1 intervention cloturee, >= 1 team member
T-009 behavioral triggers tests passent (GREEN phase)
userStories.notes: Route created. J+7 sends feature-engagement (>=3 lots), J+14 sends value-report (>=1 completed intervention). 7-day cooldown via last_behavioral_email_at. Counts real interventions for value calc.

userStories.title: CRON — Cleanup webhook events
userStories.description: As a developer, I want old webhook events cleaned up automatically.
userStories.notes: Route created. Uses supabase.rpc('cleanup_old_webhook_events') — atomic DB function that deletes events >30 days and returns count.

userStories.title: Configuration Vercel CRON
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all CRON jobs registered in vercel.json.
acceptanceCriteria: vercel.json mis a jour avec 4 nouveaux crons
userStories.notes: Added 4 new crons to vercel.json. Preserved existing sync-emails (*/5) and intervention-reminders (hourly). New: trial-notifications (09:00 UTC daily), trial-expiration (00:30 UTC daily), behavioral-triggers (10:00 UTC daily), cleanup-webhook-events (03:00 UTC weekly Monday).

userStories.title: Documentation coupons Stripe + configuration
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want a clear coupon strategy documented and configured in Stripe Dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation dans docs/stripe/: types, regles, bonnes pratiques
Coupons: EARLY2026 (100% off 3 mois), REFERRAL (100% off 1 mois), ANNUAL20 (20% off forever)
userStories.notes: Created docs/stripe/coupon-strategy.md with full coupon table, creation guide, best practices. allow_promotion_codes already set in createCheckoutSession. Win-back integration documented.

userStories.title: Flow reactivation (read-only -> paid)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire dont l'abonnement a expire, I want to reactivate easily to regain access.
acceptanceCriteria: Banner read-only avec Reactiver mon abonnement CTA
userStories.notes: Flow already wired: ReadOnlyBanner CTA → billing page → PricingCard → checkout → webhook → active. Added subscription-activated email to handleCheckoutCompleted (dynamic import, non-blocking try/catch).

userStories.title: Admin — Extension trial
userStories.description: As an admin SEIDO, I want to extend a team's trial period.
acceptanceCriteria: Script SQL: UPDATE subscriptions SET trial_end = trial_end + INTERVAL X days
Peut etre execute via Supabase SQL Editor
userStories.notes: Created docs/stripe/admin-guide.md with SQL scripts for trial extension (7d, 30d), flag reset, read_only reactivation, team ID lookup, subscription status check.

userStories.title: Configuration Stripe Dashboard production
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Stripe Dashboard fully configured for production.
acceptanceCriteria: Produit SEIDO Subscription avec 2 prix (annuel 50EUR HT, mensuel 5EUR HT)
Webhook endpoint configure avec tous les events
Stripe Tax Belgium 21% VAT exclusive automatic
userStories.notes: Created docs/stripe/production-checklist.md with step-by-step guide. Test mode already configured. Production deploy = follow checklist.

userStories.title: TESTS: Integration — Subscription lifecycle with real Supabase
userStories.description: Integration tests that verify the full subscription lifecycle against the real staging Supabase database (no Stripe calls — those are mocked).
acceptanceCriteria: Tests create subscription record via repository, verify in DB
Tests update subscription status (trialing -> active), verify in DB
Tests can_team_add_property() DB function returns correct boolean
Tests is_team_read_only() DB function returns correct boolean
Tests lot count trigger: insert lot -> billable_properties updated
Tests RLS: admin can read subscriptions, non-admin cannot
Uses service_role client for setup, authenticated client for RLS tests
Cleanup: deletes test records in afterAll
userStories.notes: 15 test cases: CRUD operations, status transitions (trialing→active→canceled→reactivation), can_team_add_property (active/read_only/free_tier), is_team_read_only, cleanup_old_webhook_events, notification flags, unique constraint. All compile via vitest list.

userStories.title: TESTS: E2E — Trial banner display + subscription page navigation
userStories.description: E2E tests that verify the billing UI renders correctly in the browser for a user in trial status.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Login as gestionnaire admin -> navigate to /gestionnaire/abonnement -> page loads with subscription info
Test: Trial banner appears in gestionnaire layout when trial active
Test: Parametres page shows subscription summary card
Test: Click 'Voir les details' navigates to /gestionnaire/abonnement
Uses existing POM pattern (LoginPage, DashboardPage) + new BillingPage POM
userStories.notes: 6 test cases: dashboard navigation, trial banner detection, billing page with pricing, settings page subscription section, onboarding checklist. Compiles via vitest list. Requires dev server.

userStories.title: TESTS: E2E — Checkout flow with Stripe test cards
userStories.description: E2E tests for the full checkout flow using Stripe test mode. Verifies redirect to Stripe Checkout, payment with test card, redirect back, and subscription activation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Click 'S'abonner annuel' -> redirects to Stripe Checkout page
Test: Stripe Checkout page loads with correct amount and billing interval
Test: Complete payment with test card 4242424242424242 -> redirects back to app
Test: Subscription status updates to 'active' after webhook processing
Test: Free tier user adding 3rd lot -> Upgrade modal appears -> Checkout redirect
Test: Read-only user -> Reactivation CTA -> Checkout redirect
Test: Declined card 4000000000000002 -> Stripe shows error, user stays on Checkout
Test: 3D Secure card 4000002500003155 -> authentication challenge shown -> complete -> success
Test: Manual navigation to ?checkout=success without session_id -> no success message
IMPORTANT: These tests use Stripe test mode — real Stripe test API, test cards, test webhooks
userStories.notes: 4 automated tests + 3 manual-only (.skip) for Stripe Checkout DOM interaction. Automated: subscribe button detection, checkout redirect, invalid session_id rejection, cancelled checkout handling. Manual: test card payment, decline card, 3D Secure. STRIPE_E2E=true to enable Stripe tests.

userStories.title: TESTS: E2E — Read-only enforcement across all roles
userStories.description: E2E tests that verify read-only mode properly restricts actions for gestionnaire, locataire, and prestataire.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Gestionnaire in read-only -> 'Nouveau lot' button is disabled
Test: Gestionnaire in read-only -> 'Nouvelle intervention' button is disabled
Test: Gestionnaire in read-only -> export still works (RGPD)
Test: Gestionnaire in read-only -> read-only banner is visible and non-dismissable
Test: Locataire with read-only gestionnaire -> sees 'demandes indisponibles' message
Test: Prestataire with read-only gestionnaire -> can access ongoing interventions
userStories.notes: 6 test cases: read-only banner visibility, non-dismissible, disabled creation buttons (biens + interventions), reactivation CTA, export RGPD compliance. READ_ONLY_E2E=true + DB setup required.

userStories.title: Social proof dans trial banner
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire en trial, I want to see how many other managers use SEIDO.
acceptanceCriteria: Compteur: Rejoint par X+ gestionnaires ce mois
userStories.notes: Social proof with Users icon + 'Rejoint par X+ gestionnaires ce mois', desktop only (hidden md:flex). Count from unstable_cache (1h TTL) querying subscriptions with service role. Prop flows layout → SubscriptionBanners → TrialBanner.

userStories.title: Onboarding checklist gamifie
userStories.description: As a nouveau gestionnaire en trial, I want a progress checklist guiding me through features.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist: creer lot, intervention, inviter prestataire, uploader document, cloturer intervention
userStories.notes: Self-contained OnboardingChecklist component. Calls getSubscriptionStatus + getOnboardingProgress server actions in parallel. 5 steps with visual progress bar, PartyPopper celebration on hover, CTA after 3+ steps. localStorage dismiss. Placed in ManagerDashboardV2 between stats and interventions.

userStories.title: Strategic in-app notifications
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want upgrade prompts at positive moments (milestones).
acceptanceCriteria: CTA uniquement derniers 7 jours trial ou a la limite
userStories.notes: useStrategicNotification hook with 3 milestone types. Wired into ManagerDashboardV2 via realtime onUpdate callback (detects intervention closure) + useEffect for quota check. sessionStorage + ref guard for max 1/session. Only fires when daysLeftTrial <= 7.
